projectSummary: A small first-release web app that helps neighborhood members browse shared tools, request reservations, and lets volunteers confirm the requests in one place.
assumptions: The first release focuses on reservation requests and volunteer review rather than payments or long-term inventory planning.
Only approved volunteers need access to the request review surface in the initial MVP.
questions: Should volunteers be able to mark a tool as returned in the first release, or is pickup confirmation enough for MVP validation?
Do members need email notifications in the first release, or can status updates stay inside the admin workflow for now?

items.title: Support the member reservation journey
items.description: Define the first-release member flow for browsing tools, understanding availability, and requesting a reservation without confusion.
notes: Keep the first release focused on the shortest useful reservation flow.

items.title: Let a member browse tools and submit a reservation request
items.description: As a neighborhood member, I want to browse the available tools and request one quickly so I can reserve what I need without back-and-forth messages.
acceptanceCriteria: A member can browse a list of available tools with short summaries.
A member can open a tool detail view and understand what the tool is for.
A member can submit a reservation request with their contact details and desired pickup timing.
assumptions: Tool availability can be represented in a simple first-release status rather than a full calendar.

items.title: Draft the catalog and request flow structure
items.description: Outline the catalog, tool detail, and reservation request flow so the member journey stays clear before implementation work is split further.

items.title: Break the member reservation journey into front-end tasks
items.description: Prepare the catalog, detail view, and request form as implementation-ready front-end tasks with clear component and state boundaries.

items.title: Define the reservation request service and data notes
items.description: Describe the minimum data fields and service behavior needed to store a reservation request and present it for volunteer review.

items.title: Support volunteer request review
items.description: Prepare the first-release volunteer workflow for reviewing pending requests and confirming which reservations should move forward.

items.title: Let a volunteer review and confirm pending reservation requests
items.description: As a volunteer coordinator, I want to review pending reservation requests in one place so I can confirm the next pickup without relying on scattered messages.
acceptanceCriteria: A volunteer can see a list of pending reservation requests.
A volunteer can review the requested tool, member details, and requested pickup timing.
A volunteer can mark a request as confirmed for the first release workflow.

items.title: Prepare volunteer review screen interaction notes
items.description: Define the content hierarchy and decision points for the volunteer review screen so the admin flow remains lightweight and understandable.

items.title: Define request review and confirmation back-end behavior
items.description: Specify the minimum service behavior needed to list pending requests and update a request to a confirmed state for the first release.

items.title: Prepare QA checks for member and volunteer reservation flows
items.description: Capture review checks for the request submission and volunteer confirmation paths so the MVP can be validated before implementation is treated as ready.

items.title: Availability status may become outdated between request and confirmation
items.description: The first-release workflow may show stale tool availability if another volunteer updates the same item outside the app before a request is confirmed.
notes: Review whether the MVP needs a lightweight lock or warning before confirmation.
